content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnacrICES CorrauIssIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (9t6) 322-0886
August 7,2006
Julia Miller
1611 New Brunswick Avenue
Sunnyvale, CA 94087
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4.-06-136
Dear Ms. Miller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the revolving door
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act").1
QUESTTON
Does section 87406.3 prohibit you from appearing before or communicating with
the local governmental agency, for which you previously held a local elected office and
where you concluded your goverrmental service on November 29,2005?
CONCLUSION
Section 87406.3 applies only to certain local govemmental agency officials who
leave their local governmental service on or after July 1,2006. Since you left
govemmental service on Novemb er 29 , 2005 , section 87 406.3 does not prohibit you ffom
appeaingbefore or communicating with the local govemmental agency you previously
represented.
FACTS
You previously served as a city council member for the City of Sunnyvale. On
November 29,2005, you concluded your term on the council. On June 8, 2006, the
family of a Sunnyvale property owner offered you a retainer fee to assist in determining
the best possible use of approximately 22 aqes of land currently zoned for residential
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. 4-06-136
Page No. 2
mobile homes. You ask specifically whether you can meet with cityplanning staff to
discuss possible rezoning options for this property, but you are concerned with the
applicability of section87406.3 to your particular circumstances.
ANALYSIS
Section 87406.3 is a new "revolving doot''provision of the Act. Essentially, the
provision prohibits certain local governmental agency officials, including local elected
officials, chief administrative officers of counties, city managers, or general managers or
chief administrators of special districts, from appearing before or communicating with,
for compensation, their former local governmental agency for the purpose of influencing
certain administrative or legislative action or influencing certain proceedings for a period
of one year. (Section87406.3(a).) However, limiting the applicability of this new
provision, the statute expressly provides for an operative date of July 1,2006. (Section
87406.3(e).)
In the Grffith Advice Letter, No. I-06-040 (copy enclosed), we examined the
question of whether the July L,2006, operative date precluded the application of section
87406.3 to a local official who left governmental service prior to the operative date. The
Grffith Advice Letter, supra, concluded that, since the local official had left
governmental service prior to the operative date, section 87406.3 did not prohibit the
local official from making appearances before or corlmunications with employees of his
former govemmental agency.
Based upon the facts you have submitted, you are a former city council member
for the City of Sunnyvale and left govefilmental service on November 29,2005.
Accordingly, section 87406.3 does not prohibit you from appearing before or
communicating with employees of your former governmental agency, as you left
governmental service prior to the July 1,2006 operative date.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
